0.q: CmDUF239-1 enhances the antioxidant capacity of grafted melon by upregulating the expression of antioxidant enzyme-related genes (CmSOD1, CmPRX53-1, CmPRX53-2, and CmCAT2) ... thereby increasing the activities of SOD, POD, and CAT

5.q: we identified 20 SOS2 genes (CmSOS2) in the melon genome... All CmSOS2 proteins contain conserved pkinase and NAF domains, essential for kinase activity and calcium sensor binding.

6.q: knocking out CmDUF239-1 reduces salt tolerance, reflected in ... diminished expression levels of related genes (CmSOD1, CmPRX53-1, CmPRX53-2, CmCAT2). Conversely, overexpression of CmDUF239-1 leads to enhanced ... gene expression

10.q: CmDUF239-1 maintains Na+/K+ homeostasis in grafted melon by upregulating the expression of Na+/K+ transport-related genes (CmSOS1, CmNHX6, CmKUP3, and CmSKOR) in the roots.

14.q: APX is a crucial component of the ASC–GSH cycle; silencing of CmAPX27 reduces the ROS-scavenging ability of melon, which confirms the antioxidant function of the protein encoded by CmAPX27.

15.q: CmDUF239-1 overexpression upregulated Na+/K+ transport-related genes (CmSOS1, CmNHX6, CmKUP3, CmSKOR), whereas CmDUF239-1 knockout had the opposite effect.
